theScene: Finally, you reach the temple at the top of the mountain, and an ornate archway proudly proclaiming 'Will-Power Temple' above the entrance.

As you look at the delicate wooden architecture with several beautiful red arches supporting an intricately tiled roof, you get the strangest feeling that you've seen something like this in the past...
Now you remember! It was from a geography class you had during your second year at Adventum, this must be a replica of the temples and shrines they have in the continents far to the east!
Sadly, you could hardly say you enjoyed the class. The instructor was the dullest person you've ever met, he even made adventuring sound boring.
And Elena said everything I learned back at adventuring school was worthless.
Smiling to yourself a little, you look around at your surroundings once more and notice another familiar sight from your academy days hidden within what appears to be a small stone garden.

It's a large blue gem gently floating in the air. A checkpoint gem, to be more specific. It should allow you to easily teleport back to the top of the mountain without having to climb it all over again!

...Even though you're pretty sure that your recall stone and grimoire would let you do that anyway. But just to be safe, you touch the floating gem and feel a rush of magic flow through you as a small blue gemstone materializes in your other hand.

You pull out your grimoire, ready to return back to town to prepare before entering the temple and notice a socket on the front cover that you're pretty sure wasn't there before. It seems to be the perfect size for your newfound checkpoint gem.
As you press the gem inside the hole, a soft magical light emanates from the grimoire. Once it fades, the gem is firmly lodged inside the cover.
Out of curiosity, you give it a few experimental tugs and find that it's firmly adhered to the grimoire.
Curiosity sated, you head back to town.
